```{r}
# Load required packages
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)

# ==========================================
# 1. DATA IMPORT & CLEANING
# ==========================================
# Load the CSV file. skip = 7 bypasses Eurostat's metadata headers.
df <- GDP_per_capita_by_NUTS_2_2007_2020

# Clean the dataframe structure
df_clean <- df %>%
  # Keep only the 'TIME' column (Region names) and the actual Year columns (2007-2020)
  # This automatically drops the empty 'Unnamed' columns in the dataset
  select(Region = TIME, matches("^20[0-9]{2}$")) %>%
  # Remove the first row which just contains the subtitle "GEO (Labels)"
  slice(-1) %>%
  # Convert Eurostat's ":" missing value markers to NA, and cast to numeric
  mutate(across(-Region, ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(na_if(.x, ":")))))

# Reshape the data from Wide to Long format for analysis
df_long <- df_clean %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Region,
    names_to = "Year",
    values_to = "GDP_Relative"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Year = as.numeric(Year),
    # Define the Treatment variable based on the strict 75% cutoff rule
    Treatment = ifelse(GDP_Relative < 75, 1, 0),
    Treatment_Status = ifelse(Treatment == 1, "Treated (GDP < 75%)", "Control (GDP >= 75%)")
  ) %>%
  # Filter out rows with NA values and drop EU-wide macro-aggregates
  filter(!is.na(GDP_Relative),
         !grepl("European Union", Region))

# ==========================================
# 2. SUMMARY STATISTICS (EVIDENCE OF VARIATION)
# ==========================================
# This table provides evidence that there are sufficient observations 
# in both the treatment and control groups to run an RDD.

cat("--- Summary Statistics: Treatment vs. Control Regions (2007-2020) ---\n")
summary_table <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Treatment_Status) %>%
  summarise(
    `Total Observations` = n(),
    `Mean GDP Relative` = round(mean(GDP_Relative), 2),
    `Median GDP Relative` = median(GDP_Relative),
    `Min GDP` = min(GDP_Relative),
    `Max GDP` = max(GDP_Relative)
  )

print(as.data.frame(summary_table))

# ==========================================
# 3. PRELIMINARY RDD DISTRIBUTION PLOT
# ==========================================
# Visualizing the distribution of the forcing variable (GDP) across regions

rdd_forcing_plot <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = GDP_Relative, fill = Treatment_Status)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 4, color = "black", alpha = 0.85) +
  # Add the critical 75% RDD cutoff line
  geom_vline(xintercept = 75, color = "#D55E00", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    # Using distinct colors for clarity
    values = c("Treated (GDP < 75%)" = "#E69F00", "Control (GDP >= 75%)" = "#0072B2"),
    name = "Funding Status"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Regional GDP per Capita (2007-2020)",
    subtitle = "Evaluating density variation around the 75% cutoff for EU Structural Funds Eligibility",
    x = "Relative GDP per Capita (EU Average = 100%)",
    y = "Number of Region-Year Observations",
    caption = "Source: Eurostat | Red dashed line represents the RDD cutoff threshold."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray30"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Display the plot
print(rdd_forcing_plot)

# Save the high-quality image to embed in presentation slides
# ggsave("forcing_variable_distribution.png", plot = rdd_forcing_plot, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```
